definition: VP CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs whose bytes progressively approach or cross a character-validity range boundary (hex-digit range, char-class acceptance predicate, or categorical state-machine transition range), enabling incremental construction of inputs that satisfy or violate range-based membership tests that binary in/out edge feedback cannot differentiate.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: With I2S held constant, value_profile's per-byte CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs progressively closer to the ICC-signature character ranges across four is_ICC_signature_char sub-comparisons, building a gradient toward the all-valid-signature word that plain cmplog lacks.
signature.one_line: VP gradient climbs toward all-valid ICC-signature word across four byte CMPs

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach png_icc_profile_error with all four bytes of the profile signature word being valid ICC-signature characters (space/digit/A-Z/a-z).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An iCCP chunk whose embedded profile-signature 4-byte word decodes to four characters each passing is_ICC_signature_char (== 32, 48-57, 65-90, or 97-122). The winner seeds carry an iCCP chunk (offset 0x35-0x38 = 69 43 43 50) with signature/payload bytes in the printable-ASCII run (45='E', 31='1'); reaching the colorspace/ICC validation path requires sustaining many byte-CMPs against ICC constants so the true side of line 1809 is taken for all four sub-bytes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog seeds have 45(E) repeated across offsets 0x27-0x33 and 31(1) at 0x3b-0x3f, with the iCCP chunk type 69 43 43 50; on the blocker line the winner takes the true side (T=8 F=16) where the loser never does (T=0 F=24), meaning its signature bytes satisfy the ICC-char range and it resolves 9/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds carry a gAMA chunk (67 41 4d 41) and an iTXt chunk (69 54 58 74) with non-signature payload bytes (00, b1, 8f, 0b, fc) at the diffing offsets; they never take the true side of the blocker (T=0 F=24) and the branch stays blocked in 9/10 trials at avg 18.78h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers already carry cmplog input-to-state substitution, so the comparison-solving mutator is held constant; the only technique delta is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. The per-byte CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs that get progressively closer to the ICC-signature character ranges across the four is_ICC_signature_char sub-comparisons, giving value_profile_cmplog a gradient toward the all-valid-signature word that plain cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only, no gradient) lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker tests whether a byte is a valid ICC signature character (space, digit, A-Z, a-z).
The caller validates all four bytes of a 32-bit signature word via is_ICC_signature_char.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: is_ICC_signature_char(it >> 24) /* checks all the top bits */ &&

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry an iCCP chunk with signature/payload bytes in the printable-ASCII run.
Loser seeds carry a gAMA chunk and an iTXt chunk with non-signature payload bytes.
Winner takes the true side of the blocker while the loser never does.
The byte diff shows winner has E (0x45) where loser has gMA/iTXt-derived bytes at the chunk-type/payload offsets.
The only technique delta between the two involved fuzzers is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: Plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, only edge-bucket signal.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
same edge-bucket signal as naive. The CMP buffer is
consumed by the mutator, not by feedback.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over cmplog with the value_profile delta.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a four-byte ICC-signature-char word where value_profile_cmplog and cmplog reach the true branch at statistically equal rates (no CMP_MAP gradient advantage), which would show the gradient does not drive the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff confirms winner has valid-ICC-char bytes and loser does not, but it does not directly prove the CMP_MAP gradient (rather than lucky havoc on the iCCP chunk type) is what gets value_profile_cmplog across the four sub-comparisons.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback scores chained hex-digit range comparisons, preserving inputs whose bytes approach then cross the hex-digit boundary, enabling incremental construction of a &#x reference with a non-hex terminator.
signature.one_line: Gradient walks bytes through hex-digit range to produce invalid hex-charref

analysis.summary_one_line: A hexadecimal character reference (&#x...) whose digit run is terminated by a non-hex-digit byte, driving xmlParseCharRef into the invalid-hex-charref error arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A hex character-reference construct that enters xmlParseCharRef's hex loop (lines 2327-2332) and then presents a byte that fails ALL three hex-digit range tests ([0-9], [a-f], [A-F]), so control reaches the else arm at line 2333 which calls xmlFatalErr(ctxt, XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF, NULL) and lands on case 256.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds carry char-ref/markup material (e.g. '(#PCD', '-UCS-4', ';link9href') and the per-role overlay marks the hex-digit range comparisons and the else->xmlFatalErr arm (lines 2327, 2329, 2331, 2333-2337) as [W] winner-only, and the blocker case 256 itself as [W]; the branch true side is taken 12 times for W but 0 times for L.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds are well-formed '<?xml version="1.0"?>' DOCTYPE/SYSTEM documents that never assemble a hex char-ref terminated by a non-hex byte; at line 256 the loser takes the false side 147 times and the true side 0 times, so the invalid-hex-charref arm is never reached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback scores the chained hex-digit range comparisons (RAW vs '0'/'9'/'a'/'f'/'A'/'F') in xmlParseCharRef, preserving inputs that drive operands toward and then just past the hex-digit boundary; this gradient lets value_profile incrementally construct a &#x reference whose terminating byte fails the range tests, while naive's edge-only feedback gives no signal on how close a byte is to a hex digit and cannot stage that construction.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF switch case at parser.c:256 inside xmlFatalErr, with the true side globally blocked.
value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in 9 of 10, a prob_div of 0.90.
The blocker is reached from xmlParseCharRef's hex loop when a byte fails all three hex-digit range tests, hitting the else arm that calls xmlFatalErr with XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF.
The winner-only overlay marks the hex-digit range comparison that gates entry to the fatal-error arm.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 12 times while the loser never takes it (0 true, 147 false).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=12 F=20  T=0 F=147  case XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF:

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry char-ref/markup fragments rather than clean well-formed documents.
Loser seeds are well-formed XML declaration / DOCTYPE documents that do not build an invalid hex char-ref.
value_profile's mechanism is CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback that preserves inputs whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses no I2S substitution mutator, so the divergence is attributable to the CMP_MAP feedback technique only.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a single isolated hex char-ref loop where value_profile and naive resolve the invalid-hex-charref arm at indistinguishable rates (no value_profile advantage), which would refute that CMP_MAP gradient feedback on the hex-digit range comparisons is the divergence driver.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed bytes show char-ref/markup material but no offset shows a literal '&#x' digit-then-non-digit run, so the exact byte path into xmlParseCharRef's else arm is inferred from the [W] overlay rather than read directly from a winner seed.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP buckets the single-byte RAW!=';' and digit-range comparisons, preserving inputs whose scanned bytes approach 0x3b or stay in '0'-'9', giving a gradient toward a complete &#digits; sequence that naive and cmplog lack.
signature.one_line: CMP_MAP gradient assembles complete decimal char-ref by rewarding near-semicolon bytes

analysis.summary_one_line: A syntactically complete decimal character reference '&#<digits>;' whose terminating ';' lets the RAW!=';' loop take its false (exit) side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A decimal character reference of the form '&#' followed by one-or-more ASCII decimal digits ('0'-'9') and then a ';' terminator, so the scanning loop at parser.c:2352 reads digits each iteration (L2359 true) and finally sees RAW == ';' to exit the loop (the globally-blocked false side).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile / value_profile_cmplog winners resolve the branch in 8/10 trials and actually hit the false (exit) side: at the blocker line the winner shows F=53 hits where the loser shows F=0, and the winner reaches L2372 'if (RAW == ';')' with F=35 while the loser has F=77 only on the true side; the CMP_MAP gradient over the single-byte 'RAW != ;' compare and the digit-range compares at L2359 steadily rewards inputs whose bytes approach ';' (0x3b) and stay in the decimal-digit range.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog blocked the branch in 10/10 and 8/10 trials respectively; at the blocker the loser only ever takes the true side (W F=53 vs L F=0) because its char-ref bytes never form a complete '&#<digits>;' that terminates the loop — e.g. loser seed 5 contains 'h&#I' where the byte after '&#' is 'I' (non-digit), driving the invalid-charref error break instead of digit-scan-then-';'.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/operand-distance bucket for the single-byte comparison RAW != ';' (and the digit-range compares at L2359). Inputs that bring the scanned byte closer to ';' (0x3b) or into '0'-'9' produce new CMP_MAP buckets and are preserved as corpus, giving a gradient toward a well-formed '&#<digits>;' that naive (edge-only) and cmplog (I2S substitution, but ';' is a constant not derived from a logged operand pair the input lacks) do not get. The delta in BOTH decisive pairs is value_profile, isolating CMP_MAP gradient as the enabling technique.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the loop-exit comparison 'while (RAW != ';')' in the decimal char-ref arm of xmlParseCharRef, and the false (exit) side is globally blocked.
The globally blocked side is the false branch (loop exit on finding ';').
Both decisive pairs have technique delta value_profile, so the enabling technique for the winner is value_profile in both.
Pair 2 also has delta value_profile (value_profile_cmplog over cmplog).
value_profile resolves 8/10 while naive blocks 10/10 at this branch.
naive never resolves the branch.
At the blocker line the winner takes the false (exit) side 53 times while the loser takes it 0 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2352  T=355 F=53  T=89 F=0  while (RAW != ';') { /* loop blocked by count */  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Only the winner reaches the ';' check at L2372 on the false side; the loser only hits the true side.
The decimal-digit range test at L2359 inside the loop is exercised, the per-iteration accumulation of decimal digits the loop scans.
A loser-blocking seed contains '&#' followed by a non-digit 'I', which cannot terminate the digit-scan loop with ';'.
value_profile's CMP_MAP encodes operand distance per CMP, and an input producing a closer-matching CMP pair adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the decimal-char-ref loop ('&#' digits ';') in which value_profile fails to resolve the loop-exit side at a significantly higher rate than naive across trials, OR per-role coverage showing naive/cmplog also reach the false side of L2352 as often as value_profile.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seeds stored at this branch are from value_profile/value_profile_cmplog (provenance) but byte diff offsets (0x00-0x0a) do not pinpoint the exact '&#<digits>;' substring, so the digit-then-';' construction is inferred from source plus the loser seed's malformed 'h&#I' rather than a clean winner '	'-style substring.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance feedback on digit-range comparisons rewards bytes progressively closer to valid decimal digits, driving longer digit runs that eventually push the loop counter past 20.
signature.one_line: CMP_MAP digit-range gradient lengthens run until count exceeds 20

analysis.summary_one_line: Take true side requires a decimal char-ref (&#) whose digit run drives the count++ loop past 20 iterations.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A decimal character reference (`&#` then a long ASCII-digit run) where each digit satisfies the `(RAW >= '0') && (RAW <= '9')` range comparison, so the loop keeps advancing through enough digits to push count past 20.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The loop body compares each byte against the '0'..'9' range at L2359; value_profile's CMP_MAP records how close each byte is to those bounds, so inputs whose bytes approach valid digits are preserved and extended, lengthening the digit run. The value_profile pair resolves 9/10 with branch hitcount 8 versus the loser's 1.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive's loser seeds reach the loop only 4 times (L2352 T=4) and average hitcount 1 on the branch; without CMP-distance feedback naive gets no gradient toward filling the run with in-range digit bytes, so the loop exits early via the invalid-char break before count exceeds 20.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds per-CMP CMP_MAP distance buckets on the digit-range comparisons at L2359, giving a Hamming/prefix gradient that rewards bytes closer to valid decimal digits. This drives progressively longer digit runs that eventually trip the count++>20 reset branch — a CMP_MAP gradient effect.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient preserves inputs making incremental progress on a multi-clause comparison cascade (character-class tests, switch type), giving a smooth climb toward the name-char true branch that binary edge feedback cannot provide.
signature.one_line: VP gradient climbs multi-clause char-class comparison cascade toward NameChar true branch

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed DTD-bearing XML doc whose attribute value reaches the old-XML-1.0 name-char check at valid.c:3573 with a letter/digit char

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Byte content that progressively matches the multi-clause comparison cascade routing into the validation path (DOCTYPE/SYSTEM keyword bytes and the attribute-type switch), so the input is rewarded for partial matches toward reaching xmlIsDocNameChar's true branch.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets so partial-match progress on the string/integer comparisons along the chain (switch type at xmlValidateAttributeValueInternal L3865, name-char clauses) is retained as new feedback, letting the fuzzer climb toward the true side and resolve in 8/10 trials (winner: resolved=8/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP gradient signal — only edge buckets plus I2S substitution — so it gets no credit for partial progress on the comparison cascade and stalls blocked in 9/10 trials (loser: resolved=1/10 blocked=9) with far fewer true-branch hits (avg hitcount on branch: winner=99 loser=8).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance feedback buckets) rewards inputs for partial comparison matches along the validation chain, giving the fuzzer a smooth climb toward the name-char true branch that plain cmplog's binary edge feedback does not provide.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback on integer character-class comparisons inside xmlIsDocNameChar rewards inputs whose leading byte edges toward a rejected-class value, letting the fuzzer incrementally synthesize an attribute value with a non-NameChar leading character.
signature.one_line: VP gradient evolves leading byte outside NameChar acceptance set via char-class CMP feedback

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach an ATTLIST NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute whose validated value begins with a non-NameChar, so xmlIsDocNameChar fails and the function returns early.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD declaring an attribute of type NMTOKENS or ENUMERATION (so xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal is invoked) whose attribute value's first non-blank character is NOT a doc NameChar, driving the !xmlIsDocNameChar test true and the early return(0) at line 3780.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile/value_profile_cmplog winners resolve the branch (10/10 and 8/10) and hit the true side (W: line 3780 [W], L3779 T=29 F=0), i.e. they synthesise an attribute value whose leading char is rejected by xmlIsDocNameChar; the CMP_MAP gradient over the character-class comparisons inside xmlIsDocNameChar lets value_profile incrementally evolve a byte that lands in the rejected class.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog losers stay blocked (8/10) and instead take the false side, falling into the xmlIsDocNameChar acceptance loop at line 3782 onward (L marks 3782-3810, L3779 T=0 F=58); their attribute values keep starting with an accepted NameChar so the true branch is never reached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile (and value_profile_cmplog) add a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance buckets; the integer-CMP comparisons that implement xmlIsDocNameChar's character-class test produce new distance buckets as a candidate byte approaches a value outside the accepted NameChar set, preserving intermediate inputs as corpus and letting the fuzzer climb to a rejected leading character. The single-technique delta in both pairs is value_profile (vpc vs cmplog and vp vs naive both add only the CMP_MAP gradient).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the !xmlIsDocNameChar test in xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal and the globally blocked side is the true branch.

signature.mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient provides partial-progress feedback over the trans-key range comparisons in the state-machine dispatch, retaining near-miss category runs that converge on the numeral-cluster case 14 transition.
signature.one_line: CMP_MAP gradient retains near-miss inputs converging on numeral-cluster state-machine case

analysis.summary_one_line: A glyph sequence whose USE categories form a numeral cluster reaching switch case 14; reached by havoc (naive) or VP gradient, not by cmplog's I2S.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same numeral-cluster category run; the differentiator is that the run is approached incrementally — partial category prefixes that the CMP_MAP gradient can reward as it climbs toward the accepting transition for case 14.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient on top of cmplog's edge+I2S signal, giving partial-progress feedback over the comparison values in the state-machine key lookups (lines 966-967, '_keys[0] <= ... <= _keys[1]'), so near-miss category runs are retained and refined; vpc resolves 8/10.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog has only edge-bucket feedback plus I2S; it gets no gradient credit for category runs that are close-but-not-accepting, so partial progress toward case 14 is discarded and the branch stays blocked 10/10 (hitcount 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner vpc differs from the loser cmplog by exactly the value_profile technique (CMP_MAP gradient / Hamming-prefix distance feedback). The gradient over the trans-key comparison values lets vpc retain incrementally-closer inputs that converge on the numeral-cluster transition. Mechanism enabling the winner = CMP_MAP gradient.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the use_numeral_cluster accepting action (case 14) inside the USE Ragel syllable state machine.
The blocker line reached is the numeral cluster found_syllable action.

value_profile_cmplog is the winner over cmplog with the value_profile delta.
value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's edge and CMP-buffer signals.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets

evidence_trail.claim: vpc resolved 8/10 while cmplog blocked 10/10 with zero hits.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The branch is reached via the trans_action switch over the Ragel transition values.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 993  T=4 F=44  T=0 F=135  case 14:  <-- BLOCKER

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness exposing the same use_category-run numeral-cluster gate in which cmplog resolves it at least as fast as naive and vpc — i.e. adding I2SRandReplace does not hurt and adding the value_profile gradient gives no incremental rescue over plain cmplog.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient acts on the Ragel trans-key range comparisons (lines 966-967) rather than just generically retaining more corpus diversity is inferential — the prompt shows the comparison structure but no direct per-CMP gradient evidence.
